unit_12 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_12

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 388 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC DOUBLE
Due to the extraordinary circumstances, British business owners found themselves in a (i)______ position during the Second World War, forced to accept interference from the government and to acquiesce to (ii)______ role for labor unions in negotiating the terms and conditions of the employment.Due to the extraordinary circumstances, British business owners found themselves in a (i)______ position during the Second World War, forced to accept interference from the government and to acquiesce to (ii)______ role for labor unions in negotiating the terms and conditions of the employment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　defensive; an enhanced
laborlabor unionunion
labor: standard unit headword | labor union: standard unit headword | union: standard unit headword
#2 SE
His own writing style was ______: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.His own writing style was ______: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.
答案：B, D　audacious; striking
strikestrikinglyricalstartle
strike: standard unit headword | striking: standard unit headword | lyrical: standard unit headword | startle: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
disseminatedistortinspired
disseminate: standard unit headword | distort: standard unit headword | inspired: standard unit headword

The message the author conveys is that the bluefin tuna fishery worldwide is being mismanaged and that despite a few __________ signs, such as the partial recovery of stocks in some areas, the immediate future looks bleak.The message the author conveys is that the bluefin tuna fishery worldwide is being mismanaged and that despite a few __________ signs, such as the partial recovery of stocks in some areas, the immediate future looks bleak.
答案：E　propitious

Constantly referring to the bold challenges and tremendous opportunities that she and the country face, the prime minister insists that she is (iii) the country, when most people would be grateful if she succeeded just in improving it a

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　overclaiming; sympathetic to; radically transforming

If a figure such as the editor and writer Pauline Hopkins presents (i) challenges to a would-be biographer because of her confounding periods of science, largely unknown personal life, and (ii) papers, the problems posed by John Edward Bruce are of the opposite kind: he published (iii) throughout his life, had an extensive correspondence, and left a huge trove of materials at his death.If a figure such as the editor and writer Pauline Hopkins presents (i) challenges to a would-be biographer because of her confounding periods of science, largely unknown personal life, and (ii) papers, the problems posed by John Edward Bruce are of the opposite kind: he published (iii) throughout his life, had an extensive correspondence, and left a huge trove of materials at his death.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　colossal; meager; indefatigably

Such (ii)_______ disappear in certain circumstances, however, resulting in a cross-pollination of our different selves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　compartmentalize; boundaries

Some of the areas of research covered in the collection have already attracted substantial scholarly interest, while others are more ______, hence requiring pioneering effort to map the territory and suggest productive avenues of inquiry.Some of the areas of research covered in the collection have already attracted substantial scholarly interest, while others are more ______, hence requiring pioneering effort to map the territory and suggest productive avenues of inquiry.
答案：B　pristine

答案：A, E　malodorous; noisome

#11 TC TRIPLE
The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity
raritybewilder
rarity: standard unit headword | bewilder: standard unit headword
#12 TC DOUBLE
Britain’s Queen Victoria, however (i)______ she had been at the beginning of her reign, was politically much more (ii)______ by the end of her time on the throne, as she resigned herself to the emergence of an increasing powerful electorate that prevented her from stubbornly insisting on getting her own way in matters of state.Britain’s Queen Victoria, however (i)______ she had been at the beginning of her reign, was politically much more (ii)______ by the end of her time on the throne, as she resigned herself to the emergence of an increasing powerful electorate that prevented her from stubbornly insisting on getting her own way in matters of state.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　obdurate; malleable
resignobdurate
resign: standard unit headword | obdurate: standard unit headword

Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very ______ of what science is supposed to be.Medieval cathedrals still stand as marvels of architecture, but as far as modern science is concerned, medieval physics and chemistry are simply irrelevant, at best a dead end, at worst the very ______ of what science is supposed to be.
答案：E　antithesis
marvel
marvel: standard unit headword

答案：D, E　civility; comity

答案：C　discounts

Blank 2
答案：C, D　provisional; useful

Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering

Blank 2

Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the _______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the _______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.

#23 TC DOUBLE
Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic
tract
tract: standard unit headword

答案：E　topple
considerably
considerably: standard unit headword
#25 SE
Circulatory systems on organisms originated in widely separated epochs, according to the fossil record, and under a broad range of circumstances; the myriad forms they take attest to that ______ of origin.Circulatory systems on organisms originated in widely separated epochs, according to the fossil record, and under a broad range of circumstances; the myriad forms they take attest to that ______ of origin.
答案：D, F　heterogeneity; diversity
attest
attest: standard unit headword
#26 TC TRIPLE
For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
absurd
absurd: standard unit headword

Each new generation of students grows up (i)______ the world of classical physics, with its mostly intuitive, billiard-ball causality, that is the everyday vantage from which we approach the alien world of quantum physics, which has for this reason never lost its air of (ii)______.Each new generation of students grows up (i)______ the world of classical physics, with its mostly intuitive, billiard-ball causality, that is the everyday vantage from which we approach the alien world of quantum physics, which has for this reason never lost its air of (ii)______.

Blank 2
答案：A, F　immersed in; radicalism
vantage

There are no ______ criteria of excellence in art: works that once were ignored or even reviled now fetch millions of dollars at auction, while those that were most highly praised in their day now languish in storage.There are no ______ criteria of excellence in art: works that once were ignored or even reviled now fetch millions of dollars at auction, while those that were most highly praised in their day now languish in storage.
答案：D　immutable
revile

答案：A, B　utilitarian; functional
utilitarian

One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)_______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)_______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)_______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)_______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event
reference

However, they simultaneously have (i)______ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii)______ such fundamental skills truly are.

Blank 2

答案：B　incommensurable

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy

Blank 3

Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii)______.It would be naïve to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer’s true state of mind, since the (i)______ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　penalty; balanced reflection

#52 TC SINGLE

答案：E　severed

答案：A, C　porous; permeable

#54 TC TRIPLE
Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　reverence for; exemplary; irreconcilable
composed
composed: standard unit headword

Orson Welles made his masterpiece, Citizen Kane, at twenty-five, and Mozart wrote his breakthrough Piano Concerto No.9 at twenty-one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　genius; precocity

答案：B　omnipresent

In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　conceal the artifice; intentional; mimicking

Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)______. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating

答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data

It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)______ her attempt to (iii)______ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)______. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)______ her attempt to (iii)______ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain
considerably
considerably: standard unit headword
#103 TC DOUBLE
Interest in creating handheld computers is fueled by the desire to shrink the size of the electronic circuitry and to create exceptionally small mechanical systems. At this scale, however, physical (i)______ poses unique challenges. Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)______.Interest in creating handheld computers is fueled by the desire to shrink the size of the electronic circuitry and to create exceptionally small mechanical systems. At this scale, however, physical (i)______ poses unique challenges. Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　manipulation; routine
pose
pose: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
Behavior dubbed reprehensible by the residents of the region is considered conventional, even ______ by those of the neighboring region; fortunately, people traveling between the two are resigned to this disparity.Behavior dubbed reprehensible by the residents of the region is considered conventional, even ______ by those of the neighboring region; fortunately, people traveling between the two are resigned to this disparity.
答案：B　compulsory
resign
resign: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Harper Lee’s narration in To kill a Mocking Bird is ______, mixing an adult’s and a child’s perspective according to no logic other than the immediate exigencies of the plot.Harper Lee’s narration in To kill a Mocking Bird is ______, mixing an adult’s and a child’s perspective according to no logic other than the immediate exigencies of the plot.
答案：B, E　a hodgepodge; a patchwork
immediate
immediate: standard unit headword
#106 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
convey
convey: standard unit headword
#107 TC SINGLE
In 1831, a domestic insurrection that had stirred central Italy was squelched by the Austrian army, but while this invasion destroyed the immediate results of the movement, it could not ______ the insurrections causes, which continued to be felt.In 1831, a domestic insurrection that had stirred central Italy was squelched by the Austrian army, but while this invasion destroyed the immediate results of the movement, it could not ______ the insurrections causes, which continued to be felt.
答案：B　extirpate

immediate: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　little errors; minor
reference
reference: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　striking; pronounced

But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)______ in ways that do not seem (iii)______ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)______ of their “little counterculture lives”, especially when terrible things are happening in the world. But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)______ in ways that do not seem (iii)______ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　triviality; resonate; inconsequential

答案：C　capricious

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

There is (i)______ evidence that the giant African land snail a. fulica is a serious threat of the spread of human disease. Giant snails do carry rat lungworm—infection by which is a common cause of meningitis—but so do many other snail species. Moreover, if the threat posed by a. fulica were (ii)______, one would expect to see an increase in disease rates (iii)______ a rise in the spread of snails. However, in places like New Caledonia, explosions in giant snail populations have coincided with a fall in the number of meningitis cases.There is (i)______ evidence that the giant African land snail a. fulica is a serious threat of the spread of human disease. Giant snails do carry rat lungworm—infection by which is a common cause of meningitis—but so do many other snail species. Moreover, if the threat posed by a. fulica were (ii)______, one would expect to see an increase in disease rates (iii)______ a rise in the spread of snails. However, in places like New Caledonia, explosions in giant snail populations have coincided with a fall in the number of meningitis cases.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　little; major; corresponding to

pose: standard unit headword

答案：B　abstemiousness

But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seem merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii) . To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii) cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i) moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Giant snails do carry rat lungworm—infection by which is a common cause of meningitis—but so do many other snail species. However, in places like New Caledonia, explosions in giant snail populations have coincided with a fall in the number of meningitis cases.There is (i) evidence that the giant African land snail a. fulica is a serious threat to the spread of human disease. #255 TC TRIPLE
A few decades from now, if the sea level rises as most scientists expect it to, fresh, high-quality drinking water will be much more (i) __________ within 60 miles of the ocean than it is now. Seawater will have (ii) __________ coastal rivers, lakes, and groundwater, turning (iii) __________ water into brackish water.A few decades from now, if the sea level rises as most scientists expect it to, fresh, high-quality drinking water will be much more (i) __________ within 60 miles of the ocean than it is now. Seawater will have (ii) __________ coastal rivers, lakes, and groundwater, turning (iii) __________ water into brackish water.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　scarce; tainted; potable
brackish
brackish: standard unit headword
#256 SE
Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.
答案：C, F　prudent; circumspect
prudent

Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. #355 SE
It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoamerica. It must have been a (i) process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii) .It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoamerica. It must have been a (i) process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii) .
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely
clandestine
clandestine: standard unit headword
#356 SE
The professor frequently reiterated a basic assumption behind the experimental method--- namely, that the outcome of the experiment is always (i) . The hypothesis can never assume the experiment’s results, in other words, but instead must (ii) their appearance.The professor frequently reiterated a basic assumption behind the experimental method--- namely, that the outcome of the experiment is always (i) . The hypothesis can never assume the experiment’s results, in other words, but instead must (ii) their appearance.
答案：A, D　undetermined; await
negate
negate: standard unit headword
#357 SE
One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious
circumstantial
circumstantial: standard unit headword
#358 SE

答案：A, D　decrying; enamored of
judicious
judicious: standard unit headword
#359 SE
Lohr’s (i) the trappings of literary celebrity creates a Romantic aura for him: by distancing himself from all public discourse about himself or his work, Lohr becomes an even greater, albeit more (ii) , celebrity than most authors manage in all their interviews and memoirs.Lohr’s (i) the trappings of literary celebrity creates a Romantic aura for him: by distancing himself from all public discourse about himself or his work, Lohr becomes an even greater, albeit more (ii) , celebrity than most authors manage in all their interviews and memoirs.
答案：A, F　refusal to wear; mysterious
renounce
renounce: standard unit headword
#360 SE
Meegan Garber argues that the typical suburban lawn, which was originally designed to (i) homes even as it enforced the distance between them, may be considered (ii) domestic space: the whole neighborhood is invested in the appearance of each neighbor's yard.Meegan Garber argues that the typical suburban lawn, which was originally designed to (i) homes even as it enforced the distance between them, may be considered (ii) domestic space: the whole neighborhood is invested in the appearance of each neighbor's yard.
答案：B, E　connect; common
utilitarian
utilitarian: standard unit headword
#361 SE
The Cassini spacecraft initially found the bodies of liquid methane on Saturn's moon Titan to be very (i) , a surprising result since the reduced surface tension and viscosity of liquid methane, as compared to water on Earth, should make it (ii) waves.The Cassini spacecraft initially found the bodies of liquid methane on Saturn's moon Titan to be very (i) , a surprising result since the reduced surface tension and viscosity of liquid methane, as compared to water on Earth, should make it (ii) waves.
答案：A, D　calm; easier to excite

distort: standard unit headword
#362 SE

答案：A, F　suggestive; coolly enigmatic
immediate
immediate: standard unit headword
#363 SE
Though extremely secretive about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (i) and was constantly (ii) information about what they intended to do next.Though extremely secretive about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (i) and was constantly (ii) information about what they intended to do next.
答案：A, F　reticence; requesting

distort: standard unit headword
#364 SE
While the United States Supreme Court has experienced changes over the years, albeit grudgingly at times, one aspect of the Court has been treated as ___________ in recent history: the notion that the Court must have only nine members.While the United States Supreme Court has experienced changes over the years, albeit grudgingly at times, one aspect of the Court has been treated as ___________ in recent history: the notion that the Court must have only nine members.
答案：C, E　sacrosanct; inviolable

pristine: standard unit headword
#365 SE
At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears ___________.At first glance, the new CEO stands in stark contrast to his predecessor: while Simpkins came across as a scolding headmaster, his successor appears ___________.
答案：A, D　blithe; winsome
judicious
judicious: standard unit headword
#366 SE

答案：A, C　sparse; paltry

circumstantial: standard unit headword
#367 SE
Although there is clear and direct evidence for the use of domestic horses in the Bronze Age cultures of the second millennium B.C.E., evidence for the use of horses before this period is ____________.Although there is clear and direct evidence for the use of domestic horses in the Bronze Age cultures of the second millennium B.C.E., evidence for the use of horses before this period is ____________.
答案：A, E　equivocal; ambiguous

circumstantial: standard unit headword
#368 SE
Despite its current stagnation, the peace process is not nearly as __________ as some officials imply.Despite its current stagnation, the peace process is not nearly as __________ as some officials imply.
答案：B, D　doomed; moribund
capricious
capricious: standard unit headword
#369 SE
Such is the strength of randomized controlled trials that the debate among medical researchers is now centered on whether there are any situations where such a trial is __________.Such is the strength of randomized controlled trials that the debate among medical researchers is now centered on whether there are any situations where such a trial is __________.
答案：B, C　unwarranted; inappropriate
onerous
onerous: standard unit headword
#370 SE
Humans' earliest interactions with fire were undoubtedly __________: prehistoric hunters may have exploited naturally occurring fire to help corral prey, for example.Humans' earliest interactions with fire were undoubtedly __________: prehistoric hunters may have exploited naturally occurring fire to help corral prey, for example.
答案：B, D　opportunistic; improvisational
utilitarian
utilitarian: standard unit headword
#371 SE
The impact of the film on people's perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to __________ history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.The impact of the film on people's perceptions of what happened illustrates the power of fiction to __________ history: patently inaccurate, the story told by the film has nevertheless come to be the standard account.
答案：A, D　trump; supplant
distort
distort: standard unit headword
#372 SE
Although penguins have a long history on the African continent, Africa has at times become __________ individual penguin species, with several species being driven to extinction before the Middle Pleistocene.Although penguins have a long history on the African continent, Africa has at times become __________ individual penguin species, with several species being driven to extinction before the Middle Pleistocene.
答案：B, E　untenable for; uninhabitable for

onerous: standard unit headword
#373 SE
Some bacteria utilize a mechanism by which they can __________: these bacteria use small, diffusible chemical signals to gauge the density of their population and initiate group-beneficial behaviors at high population densities.Some bacteria utilize a mechanism by which they can __________: these bacteria use small, diffusible chemical signals to gauge the density of their population and initiate group-beneficial behaviors at high population densities.
答案：A, E　cooperate; collaborate

disseminate: standard unit headword
#374 SE
Whether the film is __________ adaptation of the legend is debatable: the author of the screenplay claims that it was based on the original tale, but admits taking some interpretive license with the story.Whether the film is __________ adaptation of the legend is debatable: the author of the screenplay claims that it was based on the original tale, but admits taking some interpretive license with the story.
答案：B, F　a rigorous; a faithful

inspired: standard unit headword
#375 SE
Despite all the efforts of his admirers to bolster his prestige, the reputation of Alexander Pope, both as a person and as a writer, remains __________.Despite all the efforts of his admirers to bolster his prestige, the reputation of Alexander Pope, both as a person and as a writer, remains __________.
答案：A, E　insecure; precarious

pristine: standard unit headword
#376 SE
It is true that propagandists spout misinformation to serve their own ends, but if our minds were __________ such garbage, the lies would die out quickly.It is true that propagandists spout misinformation to serve their own ends, but if our minds were __________ such garbage, the lies would die out quickly.
答案：A, C　inhospitable to; unreceptive to

distort: standard unit headword
#377 SE

答案：B, D　blithe; optimistic

resign: standard unit headword

Modern historians' silence on British views of French empire-building is perhaps explained, at least in part, by the __________ of Victorian-era scholarship on pre-1870 French colonial enterprise.Modern historians' silence on British views of French empire-building is perhaps explained, at least in part, by the __________ of Victorian-era scholarship on pre-1870 French colonial enterprise.
答案：A, F　paucity; scarcity

acrimony: standard unit headword
#379 SE

答案：A, D　academic; theoretical

onerous: standard unit headword
#380 SE
Lions are __________: when necessary they will hunt large game, like buffalo and hippopotamus, but they're not too proud to eat insects or porcupines.Lions are __________: when necessary they will hunt large game, like buffalo and hippopotamus, but they're not too proud to eat insects or porcupines.
答案：B, F　adaptable; opportunists
forbear
forbear: standard unit headword
#381 SE
Noguchi had an unshakable belief in the __________ of art: he thought that artists should inject their knowledge of form into the everyday, practical designs of industry and commerce.Noguchi had an unshakable belief in the __________ of art: he thought that artists should inject their knowledge of form into the everyday, practical designs of industry and commerce.
答案：B, D　functionality; utility
immediacy
immediacy: standard unit headword
#382 SE

答案：B, E　confined; circumscribed

答案：B, D　speculative; conjectural

答案：A, D　espousing; championing

答案：C, E　eradicate; extirpate

The Tigris and Euphrates rivers are known for being ____________: they flow with such a low gradient that they meander considerably and throw numerous side branches.The Tigris and Euphrates rivers are known for being ____________: they flow with such a low gradient that they meander considerably and throw numerous side branches.
答案：E, F　convoluted; intricate
